name: GitLab
description: Monitor GitLab group security including 2FA enforcement, protected branches, and member access controls

config.setupInstructions:
1. Log in to GitLab
2. Go to your group > Settings > General > find the numeric Group ID
3. Go to User Settings > Access Tokens > create a token with read_api scope
4. Enter the token and your GitLab domain below (e.g. gitlab.com for cloud, or your self-hosted domain)

credentialFields.label: Personal Access Token
credentialFields.helpText: Found in GitLab > Profile > Access Tokens. Create a token with api or read_api scope. Must be a group Owner or Admin to view 2FA status.

credentialFields.label: GitLab Domain
credentialFields.helpText: Use 'gitlab.com' for GitLab SaaS, or your self-hosted domain e.g. gitlab.company.com

checks.name: 2FA
checks.description: Verifies that two-factor authentication is enforced at the GitLab group level

checks.name: Code Changes
checks.description: Verifies that GitLab projects have protected branches configured to enforce code review.

checks.name: Employee Access
checks.description: Lists all members of the GitLab group with their access levels and status.
